author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon VIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Of those who make accusation against an elder; and that no criminal is to be suffered to bring a charge against a bishop.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Numidius, the bishop of Maxula, said: Moreover, there are very many, not of good life, who think that their elders or bishops should be the butt for accusation; ought such to be easily admitted or no?

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Aurelius the bishop said: Is it the pleasure of your charity that he who is ensnared by divers wickednesses should have no voice of accusation against these?

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: All the bishops said: If he is criminous, his accusation is not to be received.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It has seemed good that they who are themselves defendants for crimes should not bring accusations; nor should they be allowed to lay crimes to anyone’s charge.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This is Canon vi. of Genethlius’s Synod at Carthage, a.d.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: See Canons 132 and 133 and Constantinople Canon 6.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: probably the same with senes in other canons, viz., Metropolitans, as is generally believed.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The Latin here calls them Majores natu, the Greek πατέρας.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Bishop Beveridge supposes that the word denotes bishop, though perhaps Majores natu may signify presbyters.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Justellus on the canon produces some seeming authorities for this.
